summary: Completed backend testing for bar order tracking + inventory management. All 15 pytest tests passed covering auth, categories, seeded menu/inventory, CRUD for menu/inventory/tables, and orders with tray-conversion decrement logic.

minor.issue: No items seeded for 'Alcohol' category (categories list returns 5 but only 4 have items). Spec says 18 items total which matches, but UI users may see empty 'Alcohol' category.

action_items: Consider whether 'Alcohol' category should have seeded items or whether the empty state is intentional
decrement_inventory allows negative loose_units when stock runs out - confirm this is intended business behaviour (no error/blocking)
critical_code_review_comments: decrement_inventory(): when ordered qty exceeds available stock, loose_units goes negative silently without error - consider raising 400 or warning
/api/auth/register endpoint exists but no role/admin protection - any unauthenticated user can register an account (could be intentional for public signup)
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which is invalid spec; works in this preview but should be set explicitly via CORS_ORIGINS env
Pydantic model_dump used in inventory POST without explicit field validation for negative numbers (loose_units/trays could be set negative)
seed_data_creation: No additional seeds - used app's built-in seed (18 menu + 18 inventory items). Test items use TEST_ prefix and are cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully passing. test_backend.py covers auth (JWT bearer + 401), categories (5 NL), seeded data verification (18 items, prices €2/€1.50/€2.50), CRUD for menu/inventory/tables, orders with tray-conversion (verified: loose=2,trays=2,per_tray=6,qty=5 -> loose=3,trays=1), status filter ?status=open/paid, and pay endpoint. Frontend not tested per request.
